title: carex rufa subsp. wimmeri
intro: Carex rufa subsp. wimmeri, also known as the golden sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex rufa plant, which is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, particularly due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing. Carex rufa subsp. wimmeri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
